evals.prompt: Merge my GSC and GA4 data into one funnel dataset. Use the demo fixtures.
evals.expected_output: Runs merge.py --demo, joins GSC topPages with GA4 topPages on normalized URL and GSC topQueries with Ads metrics, writes data/merged/{date}.json, and prints coverage counts.
assertions: Prints the Data Merger banner as the first action
Produces a by_url view with impressions, clicks, sessions, conversions fields
Produces a by_query view with volume, cpc, competition fields
Writes data/merged/{YYYY-MM-DD}.json with meta, by_url, by_query keys
Reports GSC+GA4 matched URL count and Ads matched query count

evals.prompt: I only have Search Console data right now, no GA4 or Ads. Can you still build the unified dataset?
evals.expected_output: Merge runs with only GSC present, by_url has GSC fields populated and GA4 fields null, by_query has Ads fields null, and meta.sources_missing lists ga4 and ads.
assertions: Treats each source as optional and runs on GSC alone
Leaves GA4 and Ads fields as null without crashing

evals.prompt: Combine search console and analytics, and explain why I cannot get per-query conversions.
evals.expected_output: Builds the merged dataset and explains the GA4 limitation: GA4 has no query dimension, so conversions are per-URL only and live in by_url, not by_query.
assertions: Triggers on combine search console and analytics phrasing
States that GA4 has no query dimension
Explains conversions are attributable per URL, not per query
